misconceptions.description: Treats a Ratio/Root Test limit of 1 as proof of divergence; L = 1 is inconclusive and requires another method.
trigger_phrases: the ratio is 1 so it diverges
limit of 1 means it fails to converge

misconceptions.display_name: Not inverting a_n when forming the ratio
misconceptions.description: Writes a_(n+1) * a_n instead of a_(n+1)/a_n (or forgets to flip factorials/powers), corrupting the ratio.
Concludes convergence when L > 1; a limit exceeding 1 forces divergence.
